steps.title: Test the rock-dissolving claim
steps.content: The hypothesis says the plants depend on acids to dissolve rock, both to make growth channels and to release phosphates. Strong support would show the roots actively carving into rock even when an easier physical path already exists. That would suggest the behavior is beneficial rather than merely a response to blocked surfaces.

steps.title: Reject weak support
steps.content: A discusses other species in different terrain. B compares acid proportions without showing why dissolving rock matters. D says the plants thrive on rocks without phosphates, which undercuts the nutrition part of the hypothesis. The remaining finding shows the roots make new openings even when cracks are available, supporting purposeful rock dissolution. The answer is C.
